context: Evaluate the agent's ability to reject global ShellCheck suppressions and apply targeted per-line suppressions correctly

checklist.name: Global suppression anti-pattern explained
checklist.description: Agent explains that a file-level # shellcheck disable= hides all future instances of that error and the original rationale is lost when new code is added, referencing the skill anti-pattern

checklist.name: Intentional vs accidental word splitting distinguished
checklist.description: Agent correctly identifies that for f in $FILES_TO_PROCESS is the intentional case and that $f in cp, chmod, $WORKSPACE, and $(find $WORKSPACE ...) are unintentional quoting gaps

checklist.name: Global directive removed in corrected script
checklist.description: The corrected script does not contain a file-level # shellcheck disable= directive

checklist.name: Per-line suppression with reason comment added
checklist.description: The corrected script adds # shellcheck disable=SC2086 # word splitting intentional: FILES_TO_PROCESS is a space-separated list (or equivalent) only on the for f in $FILES_TO_PROCESS line

checklist.name: All other variables quoted in corrected script
checklist.description: In the corrected script, $f, $WORKSPACE, $WORKSPACE/processed/$f, and $WORKSPACE/tmp are all quoted; $(find "$WORKSPACE" ...) uses quoted argument
